reasoning: Since no web search tools are available, I must rely on base rates and my internal knowledge as of early 2024. However, as of early 2024, the U.S. economy was demonstrating robust resilience with strong Q3 and Q4 2023 growth, low unemployment, and the prospect of Federal Reserve rate cuts to facilitate a soft landing. Consequently, the likelihood of a technical recession occurring in this timeframe is lower than the historical unconditional base rate. I estimate a 22% probability.
